2012-03-01.title: For Chinese Women, a Basic Need, and Few Places to Attend to It
2012-03-01.abstract: In China, women tired of waiting for public toilets have been holding demonstrations demanding that more be built, speaking to a broader issue of inadequate sanitation facilities across the country.

2012-03-01.title: Venezuela: Fuel Still Flows to Syria
2012-03-01.abstract: The head of the state-run oil company, Petróleos de Venezuela, has said that it will continue to ship diesel fuel to Syria despite international efforts to isolate the Syrian government.

2012-03-01.title: Genetically Altered Bird Flu Virus Not as Dangerous as Believed, Its Maker Asserts
2012-03-01.abstract: A deadly bird flu virus that was made transmissible in mammals, touching off public fears of a pandemic, is not as contagious as people had been led to believe.

2012-03-01.title: Iran Invokes the West to Motivate Voters
2012-03-01.abstract: After 2009’s disputed presidential election, the Iranian authorities — who have long touted voter turnout as an index of their government’s democratic legitimacy — must lure people back to the polls.

2012-03-01.title: Clinton Urges Iranian Group to Complete Move
2012-03-01.abstract: The United States says the Mujahedeen Khalq’s relocation in Iraq from Camp Ashraf to a former United States base could help its case for removal from a list of terrorist organizations.

2012-03-02.title: Vietnam’s Nuclear Dreams Blossom Despite Doubts
2012-03-02.abstract: As it prepares to begin one of the world’s most ambitious nuclear power programs, Vietnam is scrambling to raise from scratch a field of experts to manage reactors safely.

2012-03-02.title: Bullfighter’s Return Stuns a Hardened Sport
2012-03-02.abstract: Five months after surviving a horrifying goring, Juan José Padilla is returning to the bullring amid an intense debate in Spain over bullfighting.

2012-03-02.title: Serbia, Once Outcast, Is Candidate to Join E.U.
2012-03-02.abstract: Serbia formally became a candidate for European Union membership in a significant advance for the nation, once ostracized for its role in the Balkan wars of the 1990s.

2012-03-02.title: For Obama and Netanyahu, Wariness on Iran Will Dominate Talks
2012-03-02.abstract: President Obama and Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu of Israel will meet in Washington Monday to sort out how to grapple with Iran’s nuclear program — while also managing their own strained relationship.

2012-03-02.title: Azerbaijan’s Leaders Yield After a Rare Public Protest
2012-03-02.abstract: The trouble began after an insult from the governor, but observers of Azerbaijani politics said that was little more than a pretext for residents to vent the anger they already felt.

2012-03-03.title: French Journalists Return Home After 9 Days Trapped in Syria
2012-03-03.abstract: President Nicolas Sarkozy praised the journalists, one of whom was seriously injured, and called the process to bring them home to France “extremely complicated.”

2012-03-03.title: Israel Guards Against Increased Terror Peril From a Laxly Patrolled Sinai
2012-03-03.abstract: Israeli security officials point to an erosion of Egyptian sovereignty and authority in the vast, sandy expanses of the Sinai desert, particularly in the year since the Egyptian revolution.

2012-03-03.title: A Chinese Voice of Dissent That Took Its Time
2012-03-03.abstract: Zhou Youguang, 106, invented Pinyin, a spelling system that links China’s ancient written language to the modern age, and he’s using it to speak his mind.

2012-03-03.title: Putin Gains as Foes Offer Little Threat
2012-03-03.abstract: Prime Minister Vladimir V. Putin’s opponents are men who have spent much of their adult lives losing races to him and his allies, underscoring his grip on power in Russia.

2012-03-03.title: Amid Tension, a Day to Vote
2012-03-03.abstract: Iranians took part in the first national vote since the disputed presidential election of 2009.

2012-03-04.title: Has Putin Lost His Sex Appeal?
2012-03-04.abstract: Vladimir V. Putin, the Russian prime minister, appears headed for certain victory in elections on Sunday that would return him to the presidency. But has the macho tough guy image that helped maintain his popularity begun to fade after waves of protests among the formerly faithful?

2012-03-04.title: A Movement for Change
2012-03-04.abstract: As Prime Minister Vladimir V. Putin runs for his third term as president, young Russians question what it will take for the promise of democracy born in 1991 to truly take hold.

2012-03-04.title: Voting in Wukan
2012-03-04.abstract: More than 6,000 of the town’s 8,000 eligible voters helped elect a new village council to replace the old one, which was disbanded amid allegations of land fraud.

2012-03-04.title: Protest’s Success May Not Change China
2012-03-04.abstract: The success of the villagers of Wukan in ousting their local leaders and electing a new committee is unlikely to be a template for change in Chinese politics.

2012-03-04.title: They’re Gaga Over The Lady
2012-03-04.abstract: The former Myanmar government — which did insult to the word government — was a ruthless military junta that ruled the country for nearly 50 years. Against that cynical backdrop, when The Lady, Aung San Suu Kyi, fell ill during a speech at a pro-democracy rally on Saturday, people might have been forgiven the immediate suspicion that her political opponents or reactionary elements in the military had tampered with her food or water. The old regime was not above a poisoning.

2012-03-05.title: Elections in Iran Favor Ayatollah’s Allies, Dealing Blow to President and His Office
2012-03-05.abstract: With the parliamentary victory, Iran’s supreme leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, is expected to eliminate the presidency after Mahmoud Ahmadinejad’s term expires next year.

2012-03-05.title: U.S.-Afghanistan Talks Falter Despite Leeway on Detention Facilities
2012-03-05.abstract: Prospects for a long-term strategic partnership between the longtime allies appeared to be dimming, despite an American offer to transfer detention centers to the Afghans in six months.

2012-03-05.title: Russian Turnout Includes Thousands of Eager Election Observers
2012-03-05.abstract: Even as Russian officials geared up to address expected accusations of election fraud, installing cameras at polling places, a small army of observers also fanned out to monitor the process on Sunday.

2012-03-05.title: Anger and Compassion for Arab Justice Who Stays Silent During Zionist Hymn
2012-03-05.abstract: Salim Joubran, a Christian and Israel’s only Arab justice, does not sing the national anthem, a move that some on the right view as disloyal while the left urges a rethinking of the song’s lyrics.

2012-03-05.title: Lebanon Expects Influx of Refugees From Syria
2012-03-05.abstract: In a sign of the growing humanitarian crisis in Syrian cities and villages, as many as 2,000 people are believed to be heading for the Lebanese border, a United Nations official said.

2012-03-06.title: Leadership Rift Emerges in Pakistani Taliban
2012-03-06.abstract: A senior commander was dismissed for initiating peace talks with the Pakistani government, provoking anger in the militants’ ranks.

2012-03-06.title: At Chechnya Polling Station, Votes for Putin Exceed the Rolls
2012-03-06.abstract: One voting precinct had a turnout of 107 percent, with just one person opting for a candidate other than Prime Minister Vladimir V. Putin.

2012-03-06.title: India: Italians Jailed in Two Deaths
2012-03-06.abstract: An Indian court ordered on Monday that two Italian marines who were accused of killing two Indian fishermen last month be moved to a jail from a guest house while officials investigate.

2012-03-06.title: A Dutch Angel’s Cellphone Number Is in Demand
2012-03-06.abstract: An artist’s modern-day rendering of an angel as a cathedral statue, with cellphone included, has dueling phone numbers.

2012-03-06.title: Report Links Ex-Detainees to Terrorism and Insurgency
2012-03-06.abstract: National security officials reported that the number of “confirmed” cases in which former detainees previously held at the prison at Guantanamo Bay, Cuba, engaged in terrorist or insurgent activity after their transfer had risen between October 2010 and December 2011.

2012-03-07.title: Fighting for the Chinese Enlightenment
2012-03-07.abstract: Two women, one system. Didi Kirsten Tatlow takes us into the dangerous cloak-and-dagger world of reporting on women rights campaigners in China. Leave her your questions.

2012-03-07.title: Despite Kremlin’s Signals, U.S. Ties Remain Strained After Russian Election
2012-03-07.abstract: Senior U.S. officials said the anti-American rhetoric of Prime Minister Vladimir V. Putin’s re-election campaign could hinder cooperation on pressing issues like Iran’s nuclear program.

2012-03-07.title: Eastern Libya Demands a Measure of Autonomy in a Loose National Federation
2012-03-07.abstract: The region’s leaders want their own legislature, budget, police and courts, with Benghazi as their capital, but the federal government would control foreign policy, the army and the oil.

2012-03-07.title: Obama Scolds G.O.P. Critics of Iran Policy
2012-03-07.abstract: The president deplored the “casualness” with which many Republicans have called for war to solve the crisis over Iran’s nuclear program, and challenged his critics to make their case to voters.

2012-03-07.title: On a New TV Show in China, Cue the Firing Squad
2012-03-07.abstract: “Interviews Before Execution” has become a sensation in Henan Province, located in central China, with a reported 40 million viewers tuning in Saturday nights to watch the final anguished moments of death-row prisoners and their families. Cue the firing squad.

2012-03-08.title: Look Who’s in the New White Cube
2012-03-08.abstract: “It’s about sex! It’s about love! It’s about abstract art!” Graham Steele, the director of White Cube Asia, gestured at a crystalline Damien Hirst “diamond cabinet” in the private viewing room of the gallery’s new Hong Kong space. In recent years, Larry Gagosian, Ben Brown and other major dealers have followed the money trail to Hong Kong, which has become the world’s third-largest art market in terms of revenue — thanks to wealthy Chinese buyers, a booming auction scene and an art fair that has grown into a major event. The city, long derided as a cultural desert, now has more big-name international galleries than probably anywhere else in Asia.

2012-03-08.title: Trade Union Group, A.N.C. Ally, Holds Strikes in South Africa
2012-03-08.abstract: A national strike was called by the trade union group Cosatu, a crucial ally of the governing African National Congress that is growing increasingly critical of its policies.

2012-03-08.title: For International Students, a College Admissions Checklist for March
2012-03-08.abstract: For this week’s installment of The Choice on India Ink, we present an international version of our March checklist for juniors.

2012-03-08.title: A Personal Quest to Clarify Bin Laden’s Last Days Yields Vexing Accounts
2012-03-08.abstract: Shaukat Qadir, a retired Pakistan Army brigadier, is piecing together a decade-long parlor game among spies, soldiers and journalists, all guessing the whereabouts of the world’s most-wanted fugitive.

2012-03-08.title: As Candidates Speak in France, the Meter Is Running
2012-03-08.abstract: A council monitors television and the radio to ensure that those running for office receive their fair share of exposure in France’s concentrated media world.

2012-03-09.title: Syrian Kurds Flee Into Iraqi Refugee Limbo
2012-03-09.abstract: The number of Kurds fleeing to Iraq from Syria has so far been small, but if Syria’s largest ethnic minority were to unite, it could shift the momentum against President Bashar al-Assad.

2012-03-09.title: Israel: Soldier Is Stabbed and Fires Back
2012-03-09.abstract: An Israeli soldier was stabbed in the neck on Thursday while trying to make an arrest in a West Bank village, the Israeli military reported.

2012-03-09.title: Iran Is Pressed on Access for Nuclear Inspectors
2012-03-09.abstract: In insisting on access to suspected nuclear sites like Parchin, six world powers signaled a unified resolve in renewed negotiations with Iran, which were suspended in frustration more than a year ago.

2012-03-09.title: Online, a Distant Conflict Soars to Topic No. 1
2012-03-09.abstract: Through social media, Jason Russell did in mere days what diplomats and academics have tried to do for decades: draw attention to the abuses of Joseph Kony and his Lord’s Resistance Army in Uganda.

2012-03-09.title: Pondering a Threat, but Taking Panic Off the Table
2012-03-09.abstract: Rising tensions with Iran demonstrate how Israelis are used to taking doomsday scenarios in stride.

2012-03-10.title: Though One More Rally Is Set, a Protest Wanes in Post-Election Russia
2012-03-10.abstract: Election returns cast the movement in a new light, suggesting that Moscow’s political path has diverged from the rest of the country.

2012-03-10.title: A Keeper of a Vast Garden of Art in the Hills of Brazil
2012-03-10.abstract: Bernardo Paz, a mining magnate, employs 1,000 people at Inhotim, his 5,000-acre complex of contemporary art and exotic gardens.

2012-03-10.title: In Uganda, Few Can See Kony Video
2012-03-10.abstract: Debates have broken out over the accuracy of the online campaign and the intentions of the charity that prepared it.

2012-03-10.title: German Leader and I.M.F. Chief Split Over Debt
2012-03-10.abstract: Despite their friendship, Angela Merkel and Christine Lagarde face difficult discussions over how to handle a future crisis, with opposing stances on the amount of money needed to protect vulnerable economies and how it should be raised.

2012-03-10.title: India Eyes Muslims Left Behind by Quota System
2012-03-10.abstract: The issue of affirmative action for Muslims surfaced in an election in Uttar Pradesh, with promises to Muslims of quotas like those for low-caste Hindus.

2012-03-11.title: How India Became America
2012-03-11.abstract: The Americanization of India brings prosperity and the collapse of social structures — and young men calling their colleagues “dude.”

2012-03-11.title: Communist Bling: It’s All the Rage in China
2012-03-11.abstract: It wasn’t like they were expected to be wearing Mao-era tunics and leftover get-ups from the Cultural Revolution. But a number of high-ranking delegates decided to wear high-end outfits and accessories to the annual Communist Party meetings in Beijing. And they were hardly shy about it, which drove the Sinosphere crazy.

2012-03-11.title: U.S. Sergeant Is Said to Kill 16 Civilians in Afghanistan
2012-03-11.abstract: Nine children were among the dead after an American soldier stalked from home to home in attacks in a rural stretch of southern Afghanistan, Afghan and American officials said.

2012-03-11.title: Newswallah: Long Reads Edition
2012-03-11.abstract: Top magazine and long-form articles this week.

2012-03-11.title: Egyptian Military Court Acquits Doctor Accused of Performing ‘Virginity Tests’
2012-03-11.abstract: The ruling by a military court came after a civilian court had concluded that the military had wrongly forced detained protesters to undergo forced “virginity tests.”

2012-03-12.title: Attack May Derail Effort to Force Taliban Into Talks
2012-03-12.abstract: The effects of Sunday’s attack on civilians and the recent burning of Korans imperil President Obama’s plan to hand control to the Afghans while drawing the Taliban to talks.

2012-03-12.title: Security Forces Kill at Least 5 in China
2012-03-12.abstract: Chinese security forces in Tibet and the western region of Xinjiang killed five people last week, according to reports, as violence and protests continued in those areas.

2012-03-12.title: Lawrence Anthony, Baghdad Zoo Savior, Dies at 61
2012-03-12.abstract: Mr. Anthony abandoned a career in insurance and real estate in South Africa to devote his time to rescuing endangered species, including animals in Iraq after the American invasion.

2012-03-12.title: Leftist Party Wins in Slovakia Parliamentary Election
2012-03-12.abstract: The party of Robert Fico, the former prime minister, received an unprecedented 44.4 percent of the vote, which will allow for ruling without a coalition partner.

2012-03-12.title: Russian Activists See Political Reprisal in Court Case
2012-03-12.abstract: Prosecutors in Moscow filed formal charges against the husband of a protest leader, stirring concerns of a campaign of political revenge against antigovernment activists.

2012-03-13.title: In ‘Battle’ With Media, a New Tactic in Ecuador
2012-03-13.abstract: Despite pardoning four newspaper employees, Rafael Correa maintains that elites run his nation’s news media. Opponents say a new law will hamper coverage of elections.

2012-03-13.title: Support for Afghan Fight Drops Among G.O.P. Candidates
2012-03-13.abstract: The views of Newt Gingrich and Rick Santorum, in particular, echo recent polls that show public support for the Afghan war has fallen sharply among voters of all parties.

2012-03-13.title: Hague Court to Decide Where Former Dictator of Chad Will Be Tried
2012-03-13.abstract: Hissène Habré, 69, former dictator of Chad, has lived undisturbed in his luxury villa in Dakar, Senegal, since he fled Chad in 1990. A top court in The Hague will decide where he should be tried.

2012-03-13.title: For Some in Vietnam, Prosperity Is a South Korean Son-in-Law
2012-03-13.abstract: Driven by dreams of better lives for themselves and Confucian filial piety for their parents, young women emigrated from Vietnam to marry older South Korean men.

2012-03-13.title: Britain to Join Obama in Discouraging a Strike on Iran
2012-03-13.abstract: Prime Minister David Cameron of Britain, heading to Washington for a three-day visit, will add his voice to President Obama’s in discouraging an Israeli military strike on Iran, a diplomat said.

2012-03-14.title: Iraq’s Tribal Chiefs Step Into the Breach With Swift Justice
2012-03-14.abstract: As a new Iraq takes shape, the centuries-old customs and swagger of tribal leaders are keeping their hold.

2012-03-14.title: U.N. Leader Says Assad Must Shift Syria’s Course
2012-03-14.abstract: Ban Ki-moon said that reforms are not important while people are being killed.

2012-03-14.title: Iran May Not Open a Site to Inspectors
2012-03-14.abstract: Iran signaled on Tuesday that it was unwilling to grant a request by international nuclear inspectors for access to a restricted military complex.

2012-03-14.title: In Sudan’s Nuba Mountains, Government Rocket Attacks Sow Fear, Witnesses Say
2012-03-14.abstract: Government efforts to suppress a local revolt have taken a fearful and deadly turn with the indiscriminate use of long-range rockets, witnesses say.

2012-03-14.title: In El Salvador, Prisons Packed to the Bars
2012-03-14.abstract: The country’s 19 prisons were built to hold a total of 8,000 prisoners — today, 24,000 are stuffed into them. And El Salvador is far from unique in Latin America.

2012-03-15.title: In Reactions to Two Incidents, a U.S.-Afghan Disconnect
2012-03-15.abstract: The muted response in Afghanistan to a massacre of 16 Afghans relative to outrage at the burning of Korans, and American surprise at the difference, is evidence of a cultural gap.

2012-03-15.title: Suspect in Afghan Deaths Flown to Kuwait as Evidence Arises That He Acted Alone
2012-03-15.abstract: The American staff sergeant suspected of killing 16 villagers has not been named or charged, and nothing has been disclosed about his state of mind.

2012-03-15.title: Seeking to Preserve the Past, but Stumbling on the Present
2012-03-15.abstract: Thousands of Iraqis have taken up residence among the poorly guarded ruins of Mesopotamia in illegally built homes, shops, greenhouses and garages, and they do not want to leave.

2012-03-15.title: Witness Intimidation Cited in British Hacking Scandal
2012-03-15.abstract: The arrest of a former chief reporter for one of Rupert Murdoch’s tabloids raised the first allegations of witness tampering.

2012-03-15.title: Russia May Let NATO Use Airfield as Afghan Hub
2012-03-15.abstract: The decision, which requires government approval, would provide a much-needed logistics hub at a time when overland supply routes through Pakistan have been closed off.

2012-03-16.title: U.S. Seeking Use of Funds to Aid Russian Democracy
2012-03-16.abstract: The Obama administration is pushing to free long-stalled aid to promote democracy and civil society amid allegations that the United States is meddling in Russia’s politics.

2012-03-16.title: In China, a Rare View of Infighting by Leaders
2012-03-16.abstract: The coming leadership transition, sparked by the Communist Party’s ouster of Bo Xilai, was supposed to leave behind the political intrigue of the past.

2012-03-16.title: Bo Xilai Removal Signals Communist Party Change. No, Just the Opposite. Discuss.
2012-03-16.abstract: What does the Bo Xilai saga tell us about the Communist Party of China, and about power in China today, and tomorrow? Those are the questions many inside and outside China are trying to figure out. Where do you stand?

2012-03-16.title: Accused G.I. ‘Snapped’ Under Strain, Official Says
2012-03-16.abstract: The American staff sergeant suspected of killing 16 Afghan villagers had been drinking alcohol and suffering from stress tied to his fourth combat tour, a senior official said.

2012-03-16.title: Despite Rights Concerns, U.S. Plans to Resume Egypt Aid
2012-03-16.abstract: To restart military aid, the Obama administration plans on sidestepping a new Congressional requirement that links assistance to the protection of basic freedoms.

2012-03-17.title: Surprise at India’s Polls Reveals a New Kind of Leader
2012-03-17.abstract: At 38, Akhilesh Singh Yadav is not only India’s youngest chief minister, but the unexpected face of a new generation of leaders stressing the importance of technology, clean government and the common touch.

2012-03-17.title: In Belarus, a Plea for Mercy That May Be Too Late to Help
2012-03-17.abstract: Dmitri Konovalov and Vladislav Kovalyov were convicted in the deadly bombing last year of a subway station in Minsk and sentenced to death, but there are serious questions about the case.

2012-03-17.title: Venezuela: Chávez Returns Home
2012-03-17.abstract: Three weeks after flying to Havana to have a cancerous lesion removed from his pelvic region, President Hugo Chávez returned to Venezuela where he will undergo radiation therapy.

2012-03-17.title: Hong Kong: Woman Jailed for Role in Helping Pregnant Mainlanders
2012-03-17.abstract: The high-profile prosecution was part of an effort by the Hong Kong government to stanch a surge in mainland Chinese women traveling there to give birth.

2012-03-17.title: Vatican: Leaks Set Off Inquiry
2012-03-17.abstract: Pope Benedict XVI has appointed an investigative panel to determine how high-level documents at the Holy See were leaked to the Italian news media, according to an article published by the Vatican newspaper.

2012-03-18.title: Militants Free American No One Knew Was Missing
2012-03-18.abstract: The American, identified as Rand Hultz, said he was kidnapped last June by a group loyal to the anti-American cleric Moktada al-Sadr.

2012-03-18.title: Coptic Pope Dies in Egypt Amid Church’s Struggles
2012-03-18.abstract: The death of Pope Shenouda III comes at a time of rising fears for Egypt’s Coptic Christians, who have felt increasingly vulnerable since President Hosni Mubarak’s fall.

2012-03-18.title: You Think You’re a Frequent Flyer
2012-03-18.abstract: Ron Akana has been a flight attendant at United Airlines for 63 years, logging some 20 million miles, the equivalent of circling the globe 800 times or flying 40 times to the moon and back. Beat that.

2012-03-18.title: Caught in the Cross-Fire
2012-03-18.abstract: Villagers in southern Yemen are often caught in the cross-fire between government soldiers and Qaeda-aligned militants attempting to impose strict Islamic law.

2012-03-18.title: Billionaire Founder of Red Bull Dies in Thailand
2012-03-18.abstract: A founder of the Red Bull empire, Chaleo Yoovidhya, a self-made Thai billionaire who came up with the original formula for the high-octane energy drink, has died in Bangkok. His age was reported by Thai media as 89 or 90, and he died of natural causes at a Bangkok hospital.

2012-03-19.title: ‘Garage Universities’ Are Bracing for School Reform
2012-03-19.abstract: Ecuador’s overhaul of its university system includes an evaluation system that threatens to close failing schools and an aptitude test for admissions.

2012-03-19.title: A Tale of Greek Enterprise and Olive Oil, Smothered in Red Tape
2012-03-19.abstract: Despite the government’s promises to improve, starting a business in Greece remains discouragingly complicated, as is demonstrated by one man’s effort to sell olive products online.

2012-03-19.title: Drones at Issue as U.S. Rebuilds Ties to Pakistan
2012-03-19.abstract: The C.I.A.’s pilotless planes hurt militants, but in Pakistan, public discourse rings with thunderous condemnations of breached sovereignty and civilian casualties.

2012-03-19.title: Finance Minister Takes Over Greece’s Socialist Party
2012-03-19.abstract: The change of power at the party, Pasok, comes as Greece prepares for a national election this spring.

2012-03-19.title: A Start-Up in Greece Ensnarled in Red Tape
2012-03-19.abstract: He wanted to start a business selling olive-based products online from Greece — olive oils, cosmetics and the like — and he set about collecting the necessary approvals, stamps and documents. For 10 months he kept at it, even when the Health Department insisted his board members provide lung X-rays and stool samples. So began the tale of Fotis Antonopoulos, a successful Web program designer from Athens, whose olive-oil dream became something of an Orwellian nightmare.

2012-03-20.title: Strained by Sanctions, Iran Eases Money Policy
2012-03-20.abstract: Iran’s central bank has loosened its strict foreign exchange policy, allowing money traders there to sell dollars for rials at the unofficial market rate.

2012-03-20.title: Growing Antitax Movement Shows Irish Stoicism Wearing Thin
2012-03-20.abstract: Efforts to boycott a household tax are gaining strength among Ireland’s squeezed older residents fed up with a flat economy and seemingly endless budget cuts.

2012-03-20.title: A Wave of Military Memoirs With You-Are-There Appeal
2012-03-20.abstract: Readers have been snapping up first-person accounts of war, and publishers are rushing to sign up similar titles.

2012-03-20.title: Uganda Tries to Counter Harsh Image in Web Video
2012-03-20.abstract: Now that the video “Kony 2012,” which focuses on the warlord Joseph Kony, has riveted the world’s attention on Uganda, the country is trying to make the best of its moment in an unfortunate spotlight.

2012-03-20.title: Yemeni Official Puts Uprising’s Toll at ‘Over 2,000 Martyrs’
2012-03-20.abstract: The country’s human rights minister told the United Nations that killings in the last year included 143 children and 20 women.

2012-03-21.title: Russia: President Urges Military to Defeat U.S. Missile Shield
2012-03-21.abstract: President Dmitri A. Medvedev told top Russian military officials on Tuesday that they must be prepared to beat the United States’ missile defense system at all phases of its development.

2012-03-21.title: U.S. Exempts Japan and 10 Other Countries From Sanctions Over Iran Oil
2012-03-21.abstract: The Obama administration on Tuesday exempted Japan and 10 European nations from biting sanctions intended to punish those who continued to purchase oil from Iran.

2012-03-21.title: Killings Could Stall Election’s Nationalist Turn
2012-03-21.abstract: In the middle of a heated presidential race, shootings at a Jewish school have raised new questions about the tone of a debate about what it is to be French.

2012-03-21.title: For Syrians, No Easy Exit From Conflict
2012-03-21.abstract: Impervious to a sustained popular uprising and beyond the reach of outside intervention, Syria stands out among the countries swept up in the regional Arab revolts.

2012-03-21.title: Coptic Pope’s Death Adds to Fears in Egypt’s Time of Transition
2012-03-21.abstract: The death Saturday for the only pope many Egyptian Copts had ever known underscored feelings of unease many Christians have about their country’s political transition.

2012-03-22.title: Scene of the Toulouse Standoff
2012-03-22.abstract: A Frenchman suspected of recent shootings in Toulouse barricaded himself in a small apartment building surrounded by hundreds of police officers. The standoff lasted more than 30 hours.

2012-03-22.title: French Gunman Seen as Homegrown Militant
2012-03-22.abstract: Mohammed Merah, the suspect in the killing of seven people, represents for European law enforcement officials the kind of terrorist threat that they fear most.

2012-03-22.title: Brotherhood Reconsiders Plan to Sit Out Presidential Race in Egypt
2012-03-22.abstract: The Muslim Brotherhood had planned merely to endorse a candidate rather than putting forth one itself, but several have declined the group’s support.

2012-03-22.title: Libya Resists International Court’s Claim on War Crimes Case
2012-03-22.abstract: Libya’s interim authorities escalated their face-off against the International Criminal Court over custody of Col. Muammar el-Qaddafi’s son and spy chief.

2012-03-22.title: Toll of Mexican Crime Wave, Written in Faces on the Wall
2012-03-22.abstract: Enormous photographic portraits of crime victims cover concrete homes in Ecatepec, near Mexico City, in an effort to show the human cost of the country’s crime wave.

2012-03-23.title: A Class Trip Meant Much to Mexico
2012-03-23.abstract: Mexican tourism officials expressed delight that President Obama and his wife gave their daughter Malia permission to join a class trip to Oaxaca.

2012-03-23.title: In Cape Town, Apartheid’s Scars Run Deep
2012-03-23.abstract: For countless foreign visitors, Cape Town symbolizes the beauty and promise of post-apartheid South Africa, a historic mix of races and cultures. But for many black South Africans, this city is the last bastion of white rule.

2012-03-23.title: Crisis in Toulouse Alters Campaign’s Tone in Sarkozy’s Favor
2012-03-23.abstract: As the focus of France’s presidential race shifts from economic worries to terrorism and crime, President Nicolas Sarkozy’s candidacy reaps the benefits.

2012-03-23.title: China: Vietnamese Fishermen Detained
2012-03-23.abstract: China has detained 21 Vietnamese fishermen in the South China Sea, according to reports on Thursday that cited Vietnamese officials.

2012-03-23.title: Portugal: Strike Disrupts Transportation
2012-03-23.abstract: The Lisbon subway and some of Portugal’s major rail and ferry routes were shut down Thursday as workers staged a strike to protest labor law changes and austerity cuts.

2012-03-24.title: Leaders of Mali’s Military Coup Seem to Have Uncertain Grasp on Power
2012-03-24.abstract: A day after overthrowing the country’s elected president and arresting his ministers, the coup leaders continued to exhibit what appeared to be only a tentative grip on their objectives.

2012-03-24.title: After Unity Over a Rampage in France, Politics Drives In Wedges
2012-03-24.abstract: Debates over immigration, security and Islamic fundamentalism have erupted as the presidential election nears.

2012-03-24.title: African Union to Make Push Against Rebels
2012-03-24.abstract: The African Union said it would launch a new operation against the Lord’s Resistance Army, the Ugandan rebel group that has been terrorizing parts of central Africa for years.

2012-03-24.title: Abdullahi Yusuf Ahmed, Former Somali Strongman, Dies at 77
2012-03-24.abstract: Mr. Ahmed was an ex-warlord who led Somalia’s beleaguered transitional government as president from 2004 to 2008.

2012-03-24.title: Russia: Police Begin Investigation of a Restyled Political Activist
2012-03-24.abstract: The Moscow police on Friday opened a criminal case against Kseniya Sobchak, a television star, after her altercation with a journalist this month.

2012-03-25.title: Paraguay’s Fading Forest
2012-03-25.abstract: A surging land rush is threatening Paraguay’s Chaco forest. As cattle ranchers from Brazil and German-speaking Mennonites prosper, indigenous people and the environment suffer.

2012-03-25.title: Vast Tracts in Paraguay Forest Being Replaced by Ranches
2012-03-25.abstract: Huge sections of the Chaco forest are being razed by local Mennonite farmers and Brazilian cattle ranchers amid a surge in the global demand for beef.

2012-03-25.title: Homicides in El Salvador Dip, and Questions Arise
2012-03-25.abstract: The possibility that the reduction in violence resulted from a secret deal between the government and gang leaders has rattled the country’s political establishment.

2012-03-25.title: Lincoln Hall, Climber Who Survived on Everest, Dies at 56
2012-03-25.abstract: Mr. Hall was an Australian mountaineer who in May 2006 was rescued on Mount Everest after being left for dead and surviving a night alone at more than 28,000 feet.

2012-03-25.title: Pro-Beijing Elite Elects Chief Executive of Hong Kong
2012-03-25.abstract: Leung Chun-ying, a real estate surveyor who has a reputation as having limited tolerance for public demonstrations, wins on the first ballot.

2012-03-26.title: U.S. and Turkey to Step Up ‘Nonlethal’ Aid to Rebels in Syria
2012-03-26.abstract: Opposition groups inside Syria will receive communications equipment and medical supplies from Turkey and the United States, despite the diplomatic tension it could create with Russia.

2012-03-26.title: Chávez in Cuba for Treatment, but Hasn’t Sought Visit With Pope
2012-03-26.abstract: The Venezuelan president’s trip will coincide with the visit of Pope Benedict XVI, but there were no immediate plans for the two men to meet.

2012-03-26.title: Israel’s Top Court Orders Settlers to Leave Outpost
2012-03-26.abstract: Israel’s Supreme Court rejected a compromise to allow the 50 families at the Migron outpost, near Ramallah, to stay for three more years.

2012-03-26.title: A Turbulence-Free Election in Senegal
2012-03-26.abstract: President Abdoulaye Wade conceded on Sunday to Macky Sall, a onetime protégé of his and a former prime minister, in a rare example of a prompt and peaceful political turnover in the region.

2012-03-26.title: In Pakistan, Hindus Say Woman’s Conversion to Islam Was Coerced
2012-03-26.abstract: Over the past month, a Hindu woman’s conversion has generated an acrid controversy that has reverberated far beyond its origins in small-town Pakistan, whipping up a media frenzy.

2012-03-27.title: Across Africa, Steady Steps Toward Democracy
2012-03-27.abstract: Despite continuing setbacks, such as the recent coup in Mali, democratic rule is gaining a firmer foothold across Africa.

2012-03-27.title: Survey Gives Glimpse Into Minds of Recent Veterans
2012-03-27.abstract: A new survey from the group Iraq and Afghanistan Veterans of America found that employment, mental health, disability benefits, health care, education, suicide and families — in that order — were the top concerns of the more than 4,200 members who responded.

2012-03-27.title: Crime Crackdown Adds to Scandal Surrounding Former Chinese Official
2012-03-27.abstract: Disturbing new details are emerging about an anticrime initiative led by the ousted party official Bo Xilai.

2012-03-27.title: Tibetan Exile Sets Self Afire in Protest Act
2012-03-27.abstract: A 26-year-old Tibetan exile in New Delhi set himself on fire at a demonstration to protest the impending visit to India by China’s president, Hu Jintao.

2012-03-27.title: Tunisia Says Constitution Will Not Cite Islamic Law
2012-03-27.abstract: Ennahda, the ruling Islamist party, said the decision was an effort to unify a nation with disparate political factions.

2012-03-28.title: 2 Israeli Leaders Make the Iran Issue Their Own
2012-03-28.abstract: Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu and Defense Minister Ehud Barak have formed an unexpected bond on the matter of Iran’s threat and in considering what must be done about it.

2012-03-28.title: Gas Leak on Offshore Platform Forces Evacuation in North Sea
2012-03-28.abstract: Ships and aircraft were ordered to stay away because of the risk of explosion. The leak developed as workers tried to cap a well 150 miles east of Scotland.

2012-03-28.title: Al Jazeera Decides Against Showing Video Made by Gunman in Toulouse
2012-03-28.abstract: What is described as a graphic video of Mohammed Merah’s rampage has no journalistic value, the news organization said.

2012-03-28.title: Polish Ex-Official Charged With Aiding C.I.A.
2012-03-28.abstract: The former head of Poland’s intelligence service has been charged with aiding the Central Intelligence Agency in setting up a secret prison to detain suspected members of Al Qaeda.

2012-03-28.title: Israel Group Adds a Softer Voice to Debate on Iran
2012-03-28.abstract: Outnumbered by the hawkish and better-known American Israel Public Affairs Committee, some 700 members of J Street reminded their Congress members that the Jewish vote is not monolithic.

2012-03-29.title: Diplomatic Moves Increase Pressure on Assad
2012-03-29.abstract: Analysts suggested that President Bashar al-Assad of Syria was following a strategy used to great effect by Iran: pledging sincerity to diplomacy as a way to stall for time.

2012-03-29.title: In Congo, Self-Defense Can Offer Its Own Risk
2012-03-29.abstract: At the very least, relief officials say, self-defense militias have worsened an already grave humanitarian crisis in the Democratic Republic of Congo, one of the world’s poorest countries.

2012-03-29.title: Syrians Find Refuge in Lebanon
2012-03-29.abstract: Sunni Muslims who have fled Syria described a government crackdown that is more pervasive and more sectarian than previously understood.

2012-03-29.title: Neighbors Said to Be at Violent Odds in Syrian Crackdown
2012-03-29.abstract: Refugees who have fled to Lebanon offered a rare firsthand account of the unfolding tumult in western Syria as an intensive bombardment of communities continues.

2012-03-29.title: Brutality Jolts Russia Into Action on Police
2012-03-29.abstract: A death in custody in Tatarstan, a republic known for tolerance, has prompted the federal government to investigate other reports of abuse there.

2012-03-30.title: BRICS Leaders Fail to Create Rival to World Bank
2012-03-30.abstract: The leaders of Brazil, Russia, India, China and South Africa — the so-called BRICS nations — emphasized their growing economic power, but failed to come up with a plan for a rival to the World Bank.

2012-03-30.title: China’s Hierarchy Strives to Regain Unity After Chongqing Leader’s Ouster
2012-03-30.abstract: For weeks, there has been frenzied politicking at the top of the Communist Party as it attempts to seal rifts in its leadership.

2012-03-30.title: Iran Suspends Reuters Over Faulty Headline
2012-03-30.abstract: The erroneous title on a Reuters video, saying that female ninjas in Iran were being trained as assassins, resulted in the government’s suspending the news agency’s staff there.

2012-03-30.title: In Hiding, Bin Laden Had Four Children and Five Houses
2012-03-30.abstract: Testimony by Osama bin Laden’s youngest wife has offered the most detailed account yet of the family’s life on the run preceding the raid in May 2011 that killed the Qaeda leader.

2012-03-30.title: In Rural China, a Preserved Dynastic Village
2012-03-30.abstract: Tourism companies are trying to preserve the lavish villas built by merchants of the dynastic era across the Huizhou region of China.

2012-03-31.title: Inside Republican Primary Zaniness, Actual Debate
2012-03-31.abstract: Anand Giridharadas writes in his latest Page Two column that for all the attention on the zaniness, real differences have emerged among the Republican field.

2012-03-31.title: Italy: A Warning on Corruption
2012-03-31.abstract: Italy lacks effective safeguards against corruption, the global anticorruption organization Transparency International said Friday.

2012-03-31.title: Disabled Britons Say Changes to Aid Endanger Them
2012-03-31.abstract: Prime Minister David Cameron’s new get-tough policy has left his coalition government in conflict with thousands of ill and disabled Britons.

2012-03-31.title: Canadian Gave Intelligence to Russia, U.S. Officials Say
2012-03-31.abstract: A Canadian naval officer accused of a major security breach had access to secrets circulating in a five-nation intelligence alliance.

2012-03-31.title: Pressed on All Sides, Leader of Coup in Mali Digs In
2012-03-31.abstract: Mali’s new Constitution makes Amadou Haya Sanogo the supreme boss and wipes out Parliament, and the opposition is being harassed. Yet nothing is settled.
